issues.what: the-untold reinterprets D-001's escalating contracts as rising contract COUNT and PACE rather than stakes/size ('the ratchet arrives as tempo instead of tonnage'), plus rumor-silent between-roads that suppress the tale-network the ratchet runs on. The text argues escalation still finds them (fine-grained shine is shown more and sooner; the combing reads country-need regardless) and self-flags the question for the L4 napkin-sim, but as written this is the one place the locked pressure curve rests on an unverified claim: if count-and-tempo plateaus mid-run, the Untold archetype has a lawful way to stand still under a spine that forbids standing still.
issues.fix: Hold the existing in-text flag as binding: L4 napkin-sim must demonstrate the count/pace channel keeps aggregate run pressure monotonically rising through the mid-run (e.g., pace floor tied to peak-gleam, plus combing-clock acceleration from skipped hearths on between-roads). If it fails, amend the Untold creed so kettle-tier askings themselves compound in count per town, not merely in frequency.

issues.what: the-round-fair / how-the-ways-meet: 'a fair retells everything at twice the size, so the Fairwrights can raise another maker's lantern-ladder simply by singing of them.' Layer 2 pins asking tier to peak-gleam-this-run 'read from grain, never a hidden counter' and rules that delayed gleam arrivals never raise a lantern's reading until seen landing. If a doubled retelling can raise a third party's sizing directly through rumor, tier has two competing keys (grain vs. tale-size); if it routes through the retelling's caused delight running overflow back to the original maker (lawful under gleam-runoff), the lever is fine but the text does not say so.
issues.fix: One clause in the-round-fair or the-fair lever text stating that the retelling raises the ladder only via the gleam it sends home along the lines of cause (arriving on the L2 seen-landing rule) — the tale walks, the light lands, then the lantern reads.

issues.what: The Layer 3 anchor summary claims all anchors are 'declared inline above,' but the-fairwrights' own heading carries no inline anchor tag — its declaration lives only at the-round-fair's heading ('declared jointly'). A reader auditing element-by-element could count the-fairwrights as an undeclared trope.
issues.fix: Add the same '(anchor — declared jointly with the-round-fair ...)' tag to the-fairwrights heading, or reword the summary to say 'declared at the-round-fair, jointly.'

issues.what: Layer 3's foundational claim — ways keep 'no hall and no roll,' you will never hear 'a house or an order' — sits against Layer 1's 'guild-town' (spine narrative) and Layer 2's 'guild-hall wall' (the-round-of-light visual). Not a contradiction if guild-halls are town buildings rather than way-holdings, but 'guild' vocabulary is exactly the Curature-adjacent register D-003 polices, and Layer 3 is the layer that establishes there are no guilds.
issues.fix: In a later pass, rename 'guild-town'/'guild-hall' in Layers 1-2 to Roundelay-native terms (waymeet-town, makers'-hall, song-hall), or add a Layer 3 line explicitly noting the old word survives on signboards while the thing it named never existed here.

issues.what: the-eveners' hook is labeled the 'sacrifice-and-velocity archetype' while its own text insists exhausts are 'always for conversion, never for pace (the tempo exhausts belong to the Untold)' — 'velocity' here means handsel-circulation velocity, but the label reads as deck tempo and collides with the Untold's identity at exactly the seam the text works hardest to keep clean.
issues.fix: Rename the label to 'sacrifice-and-circulation' or 'composting-control archetype' so the Evener/Untold exhaust distinction survives into L4 without a footnote.

issues.what: The Fairwright entry adopts a one-sentence extension of gleam-runoff: overflow splits along ALL lines of cause and the room's share walks home slowly at a hearth's pace. Canon's one worked exemplar (Moth Fair) showed only the fiddler's share; the extension is canon-compatible but unconfirmed.
issues.fix: Confirm at L4 that delayed venue-share Standing income is modelable and desirable; if not, re-ground Fairwright gleam solely in the town-scale askings they take directly (one-line edit).

issues.what: Per D-004's threshold-adjacency rule, proposal A sat at trope density 0.14 (threshold 0.15) and the merged slate is A-based; although the merge applied every recommended silhouette/twee fix and dropped C's above-threshold material, the merged text has not itself been re-audited.
issues.fix: Run the 3-auditor majority panel on the merged Layer 3 section before closing the GATE_BATCH review, focusing on guild residue (walking-ways), fair-anchor share, and quarrel-register accumulation.

issues.what: No dedicated information archetype exists on the slate by design (light-followers stay a canon-mandated practice); information play is distributed via road-marks with the Gleaner kit leaning hardest.
issues.fix: If game-architect (L5/GDD) wants a full scry archetype, build it on road-marks + the follower practice without institutionalizing the followers (folk-scale guard is binding); otherwise no action.

summary: THE WALKING WAYS — six traveling craft-cultures, each a complete answer to 'how is regard best set into the world', worn (never conferred) and legible in gleam-grain at dusk: the Kilnfast (fired permanence; tall-permanence archetype), the Mannerly (material courtship; condition-puzzle control), the Morning-Gleaners (walk toward the grey, ripe mending; adversity economy), the Eveners (handsel circulation; conversion/velocity), the Untold (rumor-quiet roads; tempo pressure variant), the Fairwrights (the Round-Fair's one enormous public turn; declared anchor jointly with the-round-fair). Shared practices: the fair answer, red-thread days, road-marks, the quiet porch. No halls, no rolls, no violence-default: rivalry as craft disagreement and festival contest. Light-followers stay a practice threading all six, per canon. 3 declared anchors (anchor_share 0.25); archetypes mutually distinct as deck shapes. P2 recorded: the-untold reads the ratchet as tempo-not-tonnage — flagged for the gate and for game-architect to prove or clamp numerically.
